title: gymnosperma nudatum
intro: Gymnosperma nudatum, also known as New Zealand tōtara, is a type of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, native to New Zealand and Lord Howe Island. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant's health benefits also include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In traditional herbal preparations, Gymnosperma nudatum is often used in teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in topical applications like creams and ointments.
